feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://www.healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Ebola figures appear to offer hope as case numbers drop in DR Congo but it's not that simple
entry.summary: There are now 380 confirmed cases of Ebola in DR Congo, far lower than initial estimates of suspected cases, writes Fergus Walsh.

entry.title: South Africa rolls out game-changing HIV shot amid funding shortfalls
entry.summary: A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.

entry.title: This Vitamin May Lower Your Stroke Risk by 17%, According to Research
entry.summary: Learn what a narrative review found regarding vitamin D and heart disease. Plus, how to ensure you get enough of the sunshine vitamin.

entry.title: Innovation in medicine is having a breakthrough moment
entry.summary: Recent weeks have featured a series of positive developments.

entry.title: A healthier gut may be key to cancer care
entry.summary: Dubbed “the next frontier of cancer prevention and care,” the microbiome appears to be especially critical when it comes to immunotherapy.

entry.title: The World Cup vs. bugs, germs and heat: Here's the game plan
entry.summary: Despite a diminished federal presence, public health departments are preparing for common ailments that could afflict fans who gather for the event — and are keeping an eye on the Ebola outbreak, too.

entry.title: Heart transplant gives Hays teen a new lease on life; GoFundMe
entry.summary: Kaylee Stuart of Hays with her sister on May 21, days after her heart transplant at C

entry.title: My Husband Has Just Two Years to Live. He Wants Us to Lie to His Children About the Details
entry.summary: I don't like lying.

entry.title: Blueprint of a cure: The rare people who are invulnerable to HIV
entry.summary: Scientists are studying the few extraordinary individuals whose bodies seem able to naturally defend themselves from HIV in the hope of finding new cures.

entry.title: Top Doctor Demands Answers Over Trump Health Concerns
entry.summary: The White House is still being coy about the president’s big checkup.

entry.title: No red meat, no dairy, and no end in sight: How a tick-borne allergy has transformed Martha’s Vineyard
entry.summary: Lone star ticks that carry alpha gal syndrome have taken over the summer playground of presidents and celebrities.

entry.title: Popular Weight Loss Drugs Linked to Lower Risk of Breast Cancer
entry.summary: The potential benefits of GLP-1 drugs, like Ozempic or Wegovy, are stacking up.

entry.title: A new type of lyme disease found in Upstate New York according to CDC
entry.summary: A report published by the CDC on Thursday said acase of B. mayonii Lyme disease was detected in upstate New York last July.

entry.title: State health officials investigate possible cancer cluster at Uxbridge High School
entry.summary: Massachusetts health officials are investigating a possible cancer cluster at Uxbridge High School after several teachers were diagnosed with breast cancer.

entry.title: Google is releasing millions of mosquitoes in these states. See why
entry.summary: You don't need to stock up on repellent, or worry about getting more mosquito bites, these male insects don't feed on humans.

entry.title: Trump Administration Tries to Shift Blame for Ebola Response
entry.summary: After cutting its support for front-line healthcare workers in Central Africa, the Trump administration is pointing fingers.

entry.title: Weakened public health powers raise outbreak risks
entry.summary: Some jurisdictions have weakened their public health authorities in response to criticism of lockdowns, school closures, mask mandates, vaccine requirements and other COVID-era restrictions.

entry.title: People Are Not Happy About Google's Plan to Release Millions of Bioengineered Mosquitoes Into the Wild
entry.summary: The tech company has a new initiative called Debug, which plans to release millions of "good bugs" throughout the United States.

entry.title: A new blood test can help detect colon cancer, but it’s not as good as the test you hate to take
entry.summary: The symptoms of colon cancer are often silent, and colonoscopies can prevent and detect colon cancer

entry.title: Roundup: Ebola outbreak / Mosquito problem / AI doctors
entry.summary: World Cup challenge: As athletes and fans prepare for the FIFA World Cup starting next week, global health officials are gearing up to protect against infectious diseases. For the first time, the tournament will span 16 host cities across three countries, the…

entry.title: This Habit Could Lead to Early-Onset Dementia, New Study Suggests
entry.summary: Heavy drinking is linked to early-onset dementia, a new study shows. Learn how alcohol affects brain health and what you can do to lower your risk.

entry.title: Solutions Emerging for Post-GLP-1 Weight Regain
entry.summary: As real-world studies show high rates of GLP-1 discontinuation and rapid weight regain, new interventions may help patients maintain weight loss.

entry.title: Marriage sex-life advice: I just discovered the truth about the beginnings of my relationship. I’m devastated.
entry.summary: Prudie says: “that’s why I’m writing an advice column rather than running a private practice.”

entry.title: Alzheimer’s patient gets back speech, bladder control and memory in groundbreaking drug trial
entry.summary: A patient with advanced Alzheimer’s disease showed remarkable signs of improvement after taking a heroic dose of this experimental hallucinogen.

entry.title: Deadly virus now spreads to crows in San Diego — as officials issue warning
entry.summary: A new deadly virus is spreading near a prominent SoCal city.

entry.title: Google Aims to Debug California and Florida by Releasing 64 Million Mosquitoes
entry.summary: An earlier project already led to a 95 percent drop in biting females of one disease-carrying species in Fresno.

entry.title: Tainted Food Is Killing Far More People Than Previously Thought, WHO Says
entry.summary: The WHO's report provides an updated estimate on the global toll of foodborne disease.

entry.title: Ultra-processed food harms tied to content not manufacture, review says
entry.summary: Some UPFs are benign, while others are unhealthy because of their contents, according to a study published in Science

entry.title: Erie County resident tests positive for measles
entry.summary: The Erie County Department of Health reports a confirmed measles case, the first since 2018.

entry.title: Rare type of Lyme disease found for the first time in New York
entry.summary: A rare type of Lyme disease caused by B. mayonii bacteria was found for the first time in New York state. It was previously only ever detected in Minnesota and Wisconsin.

entry.title: For pancreatic cancer patients, an exciting drug can feel out of reach
entry.summary: For pancreatic cancer patients, the surge in interest for an exciting new drug is leaving them fearful about when they might get the treatment or if there will be enough to go around.

entry.title: Health department tracking possible tuberculosis exposures tied to Tri-State school
entry.summary: The NKY Health Department is trying to track down people who may have been exposed to tuberculosis

entry.title: 5 Standing Exercises That Restore Quad Strength Faster Than Gym Machines After 60
entry.summary: A certified personal trainer shares 5 standing exercises that restore quad strength, balance, and lower-body function after 60.

entry.title: Ticks are thriving in Minnesota. Experts explain why.
entry.summary: Health experts emphasize early detection and prevention as tick activity increases statewide.

entry.title: Measles Outbreak in a Child Care Facility — Lubbock, Texas, March–April 2025
entry.summary: This report describes eight confirmed measles cases linked to a child care facility in Lubbock, Texas.

entry.title: Whey Protein Doesn't Lead to More Muscle in Older Adults, Study Finds
entry.summary: A new study found whey protein alone may not improve muscle strength in older adults. Here's what actually works to stay strong as you age.

entry.title: Journal retracts study linking hepatitis vaccine to autism that was included in CDC review
entry.summary: A toxicology journal has retracted a 16-year-old study linking hepatitis B vaccines to autism in children following an independent statistical review that found a half-dozen concerns with the study…

entry.title: Researchers Solve the Mystery Behind a Billion-Dollar Dental Implant Disease
entry.summary: Researchers have uncovered why a common and costly dental implant infection often resists antibiotics.

entry.title: As Ebola Virus Spreads, We See the Terrifying Effects of Trump Dismantling USAID
entry.summary: Trump slashed more than 30 CDC experts and 140 USAID staff who managed the 2018 Ebola outbreak on the ground in the DRC.

entry.title: Opinion | Ebola is spreading in ways no one fully grasps. Use what I know.
entry.summary: As cases rapidly grow, a massive deployment of resources is critical.

entry.title: "Breaking New Ground": Man Reveals Results After Injecting Himself With Unregulated Tanning Peptide Melanotan 2
entry.summary: A viral tweet on X claimed that the man was so desperate to achieve a tanned appearance that he jabbed himself with an unregulated medication.

entry.title: What will it take to get a vaccine for the Ebola strain driving the current outbreak?
entry.summary: There is an effective vaccine for Ebola — but not for the variety spreading rapidly in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Trials are going on for several candidates. How long will it take?

entry.title: Plans Accelerated for Human Trials of Tooth Regeneration
entry.summary: A Japanese biopharma company working on a tooth regeneration medication just raised millions for a Phase 2 clinical trial.

entry.title: Your Grip Strength Says a Lot About How Long You Might Live
entry.summary: If you follow wellness channels on social media, you might've come across the claim that your grip strength – or how firmly you can squeeze something with your hands – can predict how long you will live.

entry.title: Michigan found a way to reduce school vaccine waivers. until it backfired.
entry.summary: State health officials urged parents in several counties to vaccinate babies against measles ahead of schedule this spring as cases multiplied in Michigan. The outbreaks of the highly contagious virus…

entry.title: We won’t know what screen time is doing to kids’ brains for decades, expert warns
entry.summary: Need a reason not to give your child a screen? It may permanently alter their brain during a critical period of development researchers have named the ‘criticome.’

entry.title: Golden Retriever Finds a ‘Baby’ on Sidewalk, Carries It Home & Refuses To Let Go
entry.summary: Walking past a pile of neighborhood trash can suddenly turn into a treasure hunt if you have the right attitude. That’s exactly what happened when Bowser, a...

entry.title: Cardiologist: 5 things I never do before 9 a.m.—after 20 years of treating heart attacks
entry.summary: Dr. Sanjay Bhojraj, a cardiologist who has treated heart disease for over 20 years, shares the morning habits he avoids to protect cardiovascular health and reduce unnecessary stress on the body. Instead, his routine centers on protein, fiber, hydration and s…

entry.title: Woman Has Baby Using Sperm Donor—Horror at What She Discovers Months Later
entry.summary: Sperm donor reveals woman's son has rare genetic disorder.

entry.title: The location of your body fat is linked to how fast your brain ages
entry.summary: A new study of over 18,000 adults suggests that the location of body fat, not just overall BMI, is critical to brain health. Researchers found evidence that deep belly fat is particularly damaging to the brain's white matter.
